legal_basis: Income Tax Act (ITA) 1947, Part XXB (inserted by Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2016); International Tax Compliance Regulations 2016 (G.N. No. S 393/2016) as amended
reporting_deadline: 31 May following the 31 December reporting year-end
submission_method: myTax Portal; XML upload (OECD CRS XML); also PDF form for very small reporters
nil_report_required: No - nil CRS return not required if no reportable accounts
data_retention_years: 5 years from date of collection of information
msg_ref_id_format: SG + FI UEN (Unique Entity Number) + reporting year + sequence number
doc_ref_id_format: SG + reporting year + FI UEN + unique account identifier
local_fi_scope: MAS-licensed banks (full banks, wholesale banks, merchant banks)
Holders of Capital Markets Services (CMS) licence under Securities and Futures Act (custodial services)
Collective investment schemes (CIS) constituted in Singapore: authorised and recognised schemes
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS) accounts - classified as Non-Reporting FI or excluded account (verify with IRAS)
CPF (Central Provident Fund) accounts - governmental scheme, excluded

thresholds.pre_existing_individual_high_value: SGD 1,000,000 aggregate balance (Singapore uses SGD equivalent)

local_specific_rules: SGD THRESHOLDS: Singapore converts the OECD USD thresholds to SGD using MAS prevailing rates. FIs should use the prevailing SGD/USD rate on 31 December for threshold testing.
CARF: Singapore is implementing the OECD Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework (CARF). IRAS has a dedicated CARF section. FIs with crypto-asset exposure should monitor CARF implementation timeline.

MAS REGULATORY OVERLAP: MAS (Monetary Authority of Singapore) supervises Singapore FIs from a prudential/conduct perspective. CRS compliance is primarily supervised by IRAS, but MAS may inspect CRS procedures as part of AML/CFT framework reviews.
UEN FORMAT: Singapore's Unique Entity Number (UEN) is used as the FI identifier. Format: XXXXXXXX[C] where X is digit and C is check character. Must use registered UEN exactly.
SRS ACCOUNTS: Supplementary Retirement Scheme accounts may be classified as Non-Reporting FI accounts under certain conditions. Verify current IRAS position for each account type.
CPF EXCLUSION: Central Provident Fund accounts are excluded from CRS as a governmental scheme. However, private pension products offered by licensed insurers are in scope.
MUTUAL RECOGNITION: Singapore FIs operating in jurisdictions with Singapore DTAs may rely on self-certs obtained for DTA purposes if they contain equivalent information.
IRAS E-LEARNING: IRAS provides free online CRS training modules via myTax Portal. Compliance teams should complete these annually.
PDF OPTION: Very small FIs (fewer than 10 reportable accounts) may use IRAS PDF submission form instead of XML. XML is strongly preferred for scalability.

rejection_error_codes.desc: UEN not registered for CRS reporting
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Register FI on myTax Portal before first submission
Check previous submissions; use OECD2 to amend
Validate against OECD CRS XSD and IRAS technical guide
Must match the calendar year for which FI is registered

penalties.failure_to_report: Under Income Tax Act s.105N: fine up to SGD 10,000 per failure
penalties.obstruction_of_IRAS: Fine up to SGD 10,000 and/or imprisonment up to 12 months
penalties.false_statement: Fine up to SGD 10,000 and/or imprisonment

fatca.portal: myTax Portal (same as CRS; separate FATCA filing section)
key_rules: Singapore Model 1A: Singapore FIs report FATCA data to IRAS, which passes to IRS.
Singapore FIs do not register as FFIs on IRS portal; covered by IGA.
US indicia: standard 7 indicia apply. SGD thresholds same as CRS.
FATCA filing deadline same as CRS: 31 May.

sources.notes: IRAS CRS overview page - fetched (very large page)
IRAS CRS filing page - fetched, deadline confirmed as 31 May from metadata

gaps: IRAS rejection error codes should be verified against current myTax Portal technical documentation
CARF implementation date should be confirmed when IRAS announces final timeline
SGD/USD conversion rate guidance should be checked annually
